item.title: In July of 2010 we opened a video game center.
item.description:
This part of the building was used as a martial arts studio. Was vacant when we moved in and started work in April of 2010. I remember because I quit my other job on my 29th birthday. This location is in Augusta, GA.
Signage went up late almost 2 weeks after we opened.
The tape you see on the floors were for our measurements, spacing out TV's, electrical, walls, columns, and other objects before we actually had them.
12 years ago this was the start!
Sanded and painted for over 2 weeks. If I'd known we were going to cover it completely with posters I might not have taken so much time.
This area is the front counter. Where customers come to ask for different games or if they need assistance.
Half walls just separated the action from the front and rear of the shop.
Of course we had to add lights.
This area would have everything from Smash Bros to Rock Band, currently has a N64, several NES, and a couple of arcades.
Black glass TV stands were a terrible idea. After about a year of cleaning these every day I mounted most of our screens.
This was before we opened. PS3, Xbox 360, PC, and wii.
This was a huge upgrade and made cleaning up so much easier.
We regularly fill up this space. This is an old picture but we have a day care here today with over 30 people.
Every weekend we stayed open all night 8pm to 8am. From 2014 to early 2020 right before corona shut downs were all nights 2 times a week.
Dancing, rock band, and other movement games were so much fun.
It was very common to reach capacity.
Smash Bros was a staple game.
Years ago we had some guys come in with this PC. I felt like doing something for them so I gave them one of our old PC cases!
They installed the parts right then. Probably one of my best memories.
Concessions were always the best part of the job.
Private all nights were common. 30-60 players that could play on our systems or bring their own!
Cleaning and repairing game systems is a huge part of the business.
Korean BBQ and cookouts were also common. We'd feed 100-300 people at every event!
In the coming weeks/months we are moving.
Due to the size and limitations on parking and capacity we are moving to a size that's almost double our current space!
We are attaching ourselves to a branch of the YMCA but will have our own exit and entrance.
This space is 6000 sq ft and will be completely renovated before we move in.
I've brought on several partners including the YMCA. This does several things it allows me to step back into a support role. I get to supply equipment and be more of a silent partner than have to work at the counter.
I was ready to put everything in storage or sell it off a little at a time. Later this summer most of the current equipment along with some more arcades and table top type games will all be sent about 20 miles away to open under a different name. The YMCA along with myself and my partners have a vision of esports being part of the YMCA programs. This first location is basically a test run for that part. If this first location does well I look to open at least 3 others (all attached to YMCA locations though maybe not as big as this one) with in the first 2 years. We close on the sell of our current location in 1 week. The YMCA location should be ready to go by late August, early September. I am both excited to be stepping away from the daily operations and anxious, hoping that the new managers and employees can operate the business with as much care as I have over the last 12 years. I plan to have an album showing both our move and the new place being built.
item.dc:creator: levelupgc

item.title: The Goth Girl Cosplay Collection
item.description:
Death - Sandman
Raven - Teen Titans!
Shego - Kim Possible
Lady Dimitrescu - Resident Evil Village
Bowsette - From the internet's collective brains
Marceline/Marshall Lee - Adventure Time
And... just me being a demon... Obviously my true form...

item.title: THESE. WERE. MY. EVEREST.
item.description:
It took seven goddamn tries to get these right. Over-proofing, under-proofing, broken butter, melted butter, ALL of the things. Pastry chefs and France clearly hate happiness and fun. BUT
I DID the thing! Or, le thing.

item.title: 96lb of karma right there
item.description: From The Lighter Side of Science
item.dc:creator: Polysloth

item.title: Marginalized people will suffer the most if Roe v. Wade falls
item.description: The people who will suffer the most at the hands of this are communities that are already marginalized, poor people, people of color, and immigrants. Abortion is already hard to access for these communities, and it will just make it harder. It’s wrong—alarming. The state shouldn’t be allowed to police our bodily autonomy.
item.dc:creator: Iblamemyparentstoo

item.title: Level up!
item.description:
Aww..
More awww.... Frontpage! Thank you guys *hugs and kisses* send me something nice or sweet, please.. Feeling really sad..

item.title: Minas Tirith on MS Paint by me
item.description: Finished this digital painting yesterday, took 10 hours in total. Painting time-lapse: https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Ps0Qvdr--7Y
item.dc:creator: wickedness

item.title: How do I make my Lady famous?
item.description:
Every time I post my cat, Lady, she gets a lot of attention and people are always super supportive and loving and I love the community
I rescued Lady from a junkyard while I was at work one day and at first she was skidish of being around humans but eventually she warmed up very well, she is the first cat I have ever owned!
Lady seems to be quite short but her height definitely does not match her age. She was predicted to be about 2 or 3 years old!

item.title: In 1958, Life Magazine invited Marilyn Monroe and photographer Richard Avedon to recreate images of five celebrated actresses of different eras.
item.description:
Marilyn Monroe as Theda Bara, 1958
Marilyn Monroe as Jean Harlow, 1958
Marilyn Monroe as Lillian Russell, 1958
Marilyn Monroe as Marlene Dietrich, 1958
Marilyn Monroe as Clara Bow, 1958
item.dc:creator: redspex

item.title: Just made it through probation at my IT job, here’s some IT memes to celebrate!
item.description:
Every time.
Thankfully haven’t had anyone THIS dumb, but they’re getting close…
Ha
Must be a unicorn, I always use the readme
Had me in the first half not gonna lie
Thankfully I’m still in the field but they might put me on the support desk soon -_-
Really is just more expensive LEGOs
I have fully told a user “hold on I need to google this”
The older users like to lie about this for some reason
If only they were happy little outages…happy for the guys working, not so happy for the IT guys getting yelled at by management :/
Aiming for the networking team by the end of the year, Fingers crossed
you can never pay me enough money to deal with printers only for the rest of my life, NEVER.
R.I.P internet explorer, you…won’t really be missed sorry buddy
Still not the worst rack I’ve seen
Teams, teams everywhere
Punpunpunpunpun
UNLIMITED POWAAHHH
Seriously
JPEG superiority
Or getting called over from across the building
WHY DO YOU LIE IM NOT THE POLICE
Pupper tax, just got him a month ago, lady said he was shitzu-chihuahua, vet papers say Shitzu-X, guess we’ll see what he looks like in a couple months

item.dc:creator: reservatio

item.title: The Return of the Meme (vol. 549)
item.description:
A small LOTR dump for today; I tried to do a larger dump but it wouldn't upload for some reason... anyway let's get this Lembas Bread. I know, a bite is enough to fill the stomach of a grown man, but Pippin ate half of a piece.
I'm actually reserving judgement on this. Either we will have a great thing and Silmarillion memes, or a horrible thing and Silmarillion memes AND memes about how bad it is.
Treebeard is my favorite character in common between the books and movies...
Actually a badass in his own right, as it turns out.
Fingolfin was a badass
Things I would do if I had kids. I actually got a specific hardcover version of The Hobbit back when Borders was closing down, for reading to my hypothetical children and to hand down the generations. *fart noise* I really wanted to be a dad. And I was excited that I would get to be one with my last ex, and her kid liked me and, well... nope, that didn't work. I've tried reading aloud to The Pippin, but he tends to chew on the book instead. Which I guess toddlers do...?
Gimli doesn't get the love he deserves
This happened to my friend who I introduced to LOTR. I can't remember her imgur tag.
Pippin tax! Survive today! Drink water! Take your meds! Survive today until tomorrow's red dawn!
item.dc:creator: CodyBurkett

item.title: In the USA you have Olive Garden, and in Italy we have Old Wild West, an Italian Chain that serves 'Murican food.
item.description: if you like - here's the menu https://www.oldwildwest.it/menu-old-wild-west-eng They serve Italianized Americanized Mexican food. Some of it is fascinating
item.dc:creator: psuedon

item.title: Millennial Childhood Nostalgia (VHS Edition)
item.description:
We're Back! A Dinosaur's Story (1993) A time traveling scientist goes back to prehistoric times and feeds dinosaurs a magic cereal that increases their intelligence - next they land in modern New York City for a series of comic adventures. Fun fact! John Malkovich was cast as Screweyes and recorded a full performance which was rejected by Steven Spielberg as too dark and scary. Christopher Lloyd then recored a performance which director Phil Nibbelink loved, but Spielberg rejected that too.
The Princess and the Goblin (1991) Welsh-Hungarian-American animated fantasy film directed by József Gémes and written by Robin Lyons, an adaptation of George MacDonald's 1872 novel of the same name. When a peaceful kingdom is menaced by an army of monstrous goblins, a brave and beautiful princess joins forces with a resourceful peasant boy to rescue the noble king and all his people. The lucky pair must battle the evil power of the wicked goblin prince armed only with the gift of song, the miracle of love, and a magical shimmering thread. Fun fact! The first animated feature to come to the US from Wales and was a critical and commercial failure only grossing US $2.1 million being overshadowed by the release of The Lion King. (Personally, it was one of my FAVORITE movies as a kid though.)
The Last Unicorn (1982) Based on the 1968 novel The Last Unicorn written by Peter S. Beagle, who also wrote the film's screenplay, an animated fantasy film about a unicorn who, upon learning that she is the last of her species on Earth, goes on a quest to find out what has happened to others of her kind. Fun fact! Beagle himself called the film "magnificent" in comparison to J.R.R. Tolkien's The Lord of the Rings, for which he also wrote the screenplay. (I personally will never forget how terrifying the Harpy and her tiddies were.)
Once Upon a Forest (1993) Based on the Furlings characters created by Rae Lambert, the film is about three animal children who go on an expedition to cure a friend that has been poisoned by chemical fumes. The film's environmental theme divided critics at the time of its release, along with the animation and story. Fun fact! Michael Crawford has stated he had a terrible time singing the song "Please Wake Up" as it was a grown-up singing to a child who was on the verge of dying. He had to struggle not to cry while recording.
Little Nemo: Adventures in Slumberland (1989) A Japanese animated musical fantasy about young Nemo, who goes to Slumberland and has adventures and befriends the king of Slumberland, Morpheus. But one night Nemo discovers Nightmare Land, and the evil nightmare king, throwing Slumberland and Nemo himself into danger. Fun fact! Nemo became the #4 best-selling children's title of 1993, with 1.5 million copies sold, despite being a massive box office bomb.
The Sea Prince and the Fire Child (1981) A Japanese anime film said to be a loose retelling of Romeo and Juliet, itself a European version of many tales of star-crossed lovers in a variety of cultures and eras. In this story, the children of the gods of fire and water fall in love and fight to stay together in the face of adversity. Fun fact! I actually thought this movie was a fever dream beause it was impossible to find prior to modern internet. The copy I used to watch as a kid was somehow taped on a home movie VHS.
The Pebble and the Penguin (1995) Based on the true life mating rituals of the Adélie penguins in Antarctica, the film focuses on a timid, stuttering penguin named Hubie who tries to impress a beautiful penguin named Marina by giving her a pebble that fell from the sky and keep her from the clutches of an evil penguin named Drake who wants Marina for himself. Fun fact! Producer/Co-director Don Bluth so hated the final results of this movie after production was wrapped up that he and his partner, co-director Gary Goldman, demanded to be uncredited.
The Secret of NIMH (1982) To save her ill son, a field mouse must seek the aid of a colony of rats, with whom she has a deeper link than she suspected. Fun fact! Don Bluth, John Pomeroy, and Gary Goldman all left Disney to pursue this project, which had originally been rejected by their former employer as "too dark" to be a commercial success. They were followed soon after by twenty other Disney animators, dubbed "The Disney Defectors" by the trade press.
The Swan Princess (1994) An American animated musical fantasy film based on the ballet Swan Lake. A beautiful princess falls in love with a prince from another land, but an exiled magician who wants her for himself conjures up a spell to transform her into a swan. Forced to embark on a dangerous quest to reverse the magic, the princess is helped along by some new-found animal friends. Fun fact! John Cleese had a choice of playing Zazu in The Lion King (1994) or Jean-Bob in this film. He chose to play Jean-Bob because he felt this character was more interesting.
The Brave Little Toaster (1987) Five sentient household appliances living in an abandoned cabin have grown lonely. Longing to reunite with their owner the appliances set off on a harrowing trek through the wilderness, surviving as best they can despite limited sources of electricity. Worse yet, when they arrive in the big city they discover they may no longer be useful because of new, modern appliances. Fun fact! This film is unique in that it attracted a substantial amount of talent from both old and new sources. Many of the cast and crew members went on to have successful careers in the animation industry. Co-writer Joe Ranft became a script supervisor at Pixar, while animators Glen Keane, Kirk Wise and Kevin Lima went on to animate and co-direct films of the Disney Renaissance.
An American Tail (1986) A young mouse named Fievel Mousekewitz and his family emigrate from Russia to the United States by boat after their home is destroyed by cats. During the trip, a fierce storm throws Fievel from the ship, and he loses contact with his family. Luckily, he manages to sail to New York in a bottle. There, Irish mouse Bridget, an Italian mouse named Tony and a kindly cat named Tiger help Fievel search for his loved ones. Fun fact! The scene in which Fievel presses up against a window to look into a classroom filled with American "schoolmice" is based on a story Steven Spielberg remembered about his grandfather, who told him that Jews were only able to listen to school lessons through open windows while sitting outside in the snow.
A Troll in Central Park (1994) Stanley is a kindhearted, popular troll who can create flowers with a mere touch. When Gnorga , the mean-spirited Queen of the Kingdom of Trolls discovers Stanley's secret, she banishes him to a faraway place where she assumes nothing green can grow - New York City. Fun fact! The film holds an approval rating of 14% with an average of 3.48 out of 10 and is widely considered to be Don Bluth's worst film.
Thumbelina (1994) Born of a flower and growing to only a couple of inches tall, poor Thumbelina is worried she'll never meet someone her own size, until she happens to catch the eye of Prince Cornelius of the Fairies. Just as soon as she finds love, however, it's torn away from her when she is kidnapped by Ms. Toad. Now Thumbelina has to escape Ms. Toad's grasp and search for Prince Cornelius. Luckily, there's a whole city of animals willing to help her. Fun fact! The film was a commercial failure, grossing $11.4 million at the US and Canadian box office. The film reportedly received higher scores during test screenings, where Warner Bros. replaced their logo with that of Walt Disney Pictures.
The Goonies (1985) A group of young misfits called The Goonies discover an ancient map and set out on an adventure to find a legendary pirate's long-lost treasure. Fun fact! The pirate ship was entirely real. All the shots were filmed in the ship. After the film, it was offered to anyone who would take it. No one wanted it, so the ship was scrapped.
Willow (1988) Willow, a small farmer/apprentice magician, meets Madmartigan, a great swordsman, and together they journey through a war-torn land of magic and monsters, to save a baby princess from death at the hands of an evil queen. Fun fact! According to Warwick Davis, the film had the largest ever casting call for little people at the time. Between 225 and 240 actors were hired for the film. He was only 17 years old at the time of shooting.
Labyrinth (1986) Sixteen-year-old Sarah is given thirteen hours to solve a labyrinth and rescue her baby brother Toby when her wish for him to be taken away is granted by the Goblin King Jareth. I don't think there is a person alive who has seen this movie that doesn't vividly remember David Bowie's junk. Fun fact! The various things that Jareth does with the crystal balls are not camera tricks or any other kind of special effect and are actually done by choreographer Michael Moschen, who is an accomplished juggler. Moschen was actually crouched behind David Bowie with his arms replacing Bowie's. His manipulations were performed completely blind.
The Pagemaster (1994) A cowardly boy, who buries himself in accident statistics, enters a library to escape a storm, only to be transformed into an animated illustration by the Pagemaster. He has to work through obstacles from classic books to return to real-life. Fun fact! This movie took almost three and a half years to complete.
FernGully: The Last Rainforest (1992) Crysta is a fairy who lives in FernGully, a rainforest in Australia, and has never seen a human before. In fact, she is told they are extinct. But when a logging company comes near the rainforest, she sees that they do exist, and even accidentally shrinks one of them: a boy named Zak. Now her size, Zak sees the damage that the company does and helps Crysta to stop not only them, but an evil entity named Hexxus, who feeds off pollution. Fun fact! The original lyrics to the movie's song "Toxic Love", performed by Tim Curry, had obvious sexual themes/implications, and various verses from the original song were cut and or re-written to make the song more appropriate for a family movie. The original lyrics can still be found, but the song's full version was never used.
Casper (1995) An afterlife therapist and his daughter meet a friendly young ghost when they move into a crumbling mansion in order to rid the premises of wicked spirits. Fun fact! This is the first feature film to have a fully computer-generated visual effects character in a leading role.
Hans Christian Andersen's The Little Mermaid (1975) A Japanese anime film based on Hans Christian Andersen's 1837 eponymous fairy tale. A mermaid princess sees a handsome earthly prince, falls in love with him, and longs to become human. Fun fact! Unlike the Disney adaptation released 14 years later, this film is closer to Andersen's story, notably in its preservation of the original and tragic ending.
All Dogs go to Heaven (1989) A canine angel, Charlie, sneaks back to earth from heaven but ends up befriending an orphan girl who can speak to animals. In the process, Charlie learns that friendship is the most heavenly gift of all. Not so fun fact! This was Judith Barsi's final film. She and her mother were murdered by Barsi's father a year and a half before the film's release. The ending theme, "Love Survives," is dedicated to Barsi's memory.
Oliver & Company (1988) Oliver is an orphaned kitten taken in by a gang of thieving dogs, led by cavalier canine Dodger and owned by down-and-out pickpocket Fagin. While pulling a job in the streets of New York City, Oliver winds up being adopted by a rich girl, Jenny, and landing on easy street. But through a series of events, a loan shark threatens the peaceful new arrangement. Fun fact! Animators shot photos of New York streets as reference, using cameras set eighteen inches off the ground to get a dog's point of view.
Cats Don't Dance (1997) Danny is trying to break into 1930s Hollywood but he's a cat and he can't understand why he's only getting parts playing an animal. He gets into trouble with a human star and almost sees his chance at a movie career disappear. Fun fact! The film was launched as a vehicle for Michael Jackson, who would produce, star, and be a consultant in the music and choreography. It would have been a hybrid live-action/CGI film. However, by 1994, Jackson had ceased to be involved.
The Prince of Egypt (1998) In this animated retelling of the Book of Exodus, Egyptian Prince Moses, upon discovering his roots as a Jewish slave, embarks on a quest to free his people from bondage. Fun fact! The four-minute parting of the Red Sea sequence took ten animators two years to complete.
Rover Dangerfield (1991) Starring the voice talent of comedian Rodney Dangerfield, a show dog from Las Vegas gets ditched in the sticks after his owner's boyfriend throws him out of the house. The carefree canine gets a taste of the rough life and ends up working on a farm. Fun fact! The movie was originally planned to be an R-rated animated film but Warner Brothers wanted the film toned down to earn a G-rating.
The NeverEnding Story (1984) Bastian is a young boy who lives a dreary life being tormented by school bullies. On one such occasion he escapes into a book shop where the old proprieter reveals an ancient story-book to him, which he is warned can be dangerous. Shortly after, he "borrows" the book and begins to read it in the school attic where he is drawn into the mythical land of Fantasia, which desperately needs a hero to save it from destruction. Fun fact! At the time of its release, The NeverEnding Story was the most expensive film production in the history of German cinema. In additon, the original author of the book actually despised the movie.
The Indian in the Cupboard (1995) Omri, a young boy growing up in Brooklyn, receives an odd variety of presents for his birthday: a wooden cupboard from his older brother, a set of antique keys from his mother, and a plastic Indian from his best friend, Patrick. Fun fact! Originally, this was intended to be the first installment of a tetra-logy, but the poor box office performance resulted in plans to adapt the next three books in the series being canceled.
The Parent Trap (1998) Identical twins Annie and Hallie, separated at birth and each raised by one of their biological parents, discover each other for the first time at summer camp and make a plan to bring their wayward parents back together. Fun fact! When Lindsay Lohan was 11 she pretended to be ill so she could skip school and go to her first movie audition for The Parent Trap. This was her first real film.
Anastasia (1997) The evil wizard Rasputin puts a hex on the royal Romanovs and young Anastasia (Meg disappears when their palace is overrun. Years later, the Grand Duchess offers a reward for Anastasia's return. Two scheming Russians planning to pawn off a phony, hold auditions and choose an orphan girl with a remarkable resemblance to the missing princess. They bring her to Paris for the reward, not knowing she's the real Anastasia. Fun fact! In real life, Olga really did say that Anastasia's drawing looked like a pig riding a donkey. This was stated by Anastasia in a letter to her father, and the image used in the movie is a reproduction of the original picture.
James and the Giant Peach (1996) An orphan who lives with his two cruel aunts befriends anthropomorphic bugs who live inside a giant peach, and they embark on a journey to New York City. Fun fact! This film took twelve years to reach the screen. Roald Dahl himself was convinced that the property would never make a viable film.
Jumanji (1995) A magical board game unleashes a world of adventure on siblings Peter and Judy Shepherd. While exploring an old mansion, the youngsters find a curious, jungle-themed game called Jumanji in the attic. When they start playing, they free Alan Parrish, who's been stuck in the game's inner world for decades. If they win Jumanji, the kids can free Alan for good -- but that means braving giant bugs, ill-mannered monkeys and even stampeding rhinos! Fun fact! Robin Williams was beloved by Keene, New Hampshire townsfolk during filming. He was even presented with the keys to the city by Keene's mayor in 1994. After his death in 2014, Keene residents crafted a makeshift memorial of flowers and candles below the Parrish Shoes sign, and even organized a public screening of the film.
Grimm's Fairy Tale Classics (1988) A Japanese anime anthology series by Nippon Animation. The episodes are adaptations of the Grimms' Fairy Tales. Most of the tales were presented in one episode, while some stories in the first season were told over two or four episodes, for a total of 41 fairy tales. The fairy tale anthology was broadcast in the United States by Nickelodeon, in local stations throughout Europe, Latin America, Philippines, Israel, Arab World, New Zealand and Australia. It took me forever to figure out what this anthology actually was to post it here. It's rather hard to find when all you have is a childhood memory. The Worn Out Dancing Shoes episode was a personal favorite and there was also an episode about the demon Beelzebub with animation that was rather horrifying.
Matilda (1996) A girl gifted with a keen intellect and psychic powers uses both to deal with her crude, distant family and free her kind teacher from their sadistic headmistress. Fun fact! In stark contrast to their characters, Danny DeVito and Rhea Perlman became very close to Mara Wilson during production. According to Wilson, DeVito and Perlman would often let her stay at their home and keep her entertained while her father was working and her mother was in the hospital receiving cancer treatments.
E.T. the Extra-Terrestrial (1982) After a gentle alien becomes stranded on Earth, the being is discovered and befriended by a young boy named Elliott. Soon, however, E.T. falls ill, resulting in government intervention and a dire situation for both Elliott and the alien. Fun fact! Most of the full-body puppetry was performed by a 2'10" tall stuntman, but the scenes in the kitchen were done using a 12-year-old boy who was born without legs but was an expert on walking on his hands.
Willy Wonka & the Chocolate Factory (1971) A poor but hopeful boy seeks one of the five coveted golden tickets that will send him on a tour of Willy Wonka's mysterious chocolate factory. Fun fact! The reactions of the actors and actresses in some scenes are spontaneous. For example, when the children first enter the Chocolate Room and see the candy gardens, their reactions are genuine.
The Iron Giant (1999) A giant alien robot crash-lands near the small town of Rockwell, Maine, in 1957. Exploring the area, a local 9-year-old boy, Hogarth, discovers the robot, and soon forms an unlikely friendship with him. When a paranoid government agent becomes determined to destroy the robot, Hogarth he can to save the misunderstood machine. Fun fact! Brad Bird was in part inspired to make this film as a memorial to his sister Susan, who died at the hands of her estranged husband by gun violence. His pitch was this: "What if a gun had a soul and didn't want to be a gun?"
The Adventures of Milo and Otis (1986) The story of two animals and their adventures. Milo, the cat, and Otis, the dog, are two animals who grew up together on the same farm. One day, the two are separated and begin a journey to find each other. The adventurous, and often perilous quest finds the two animals traveling across mountains, plains, and snow-covered lands searching for one another. Not so fun fact! This film has been controversial since its release due to allegations of animal abuse, including claims that a number of kittens were killed during filming. Animal advocacy groups were not allowed to supervise during production, so those reports can't be confirmed. However, many have noted that the dangerous conditions the animals were placed in (including footage of a cat falling off of a cliff) were likely due to the filmmakers intentionally putting the animals into those situations.
Teenage Mutant Ninja Turtles (1990) Through contact with a mysterious ooze, four turtles in the sewers of New York mutate into intelligent pizza-loving humanoids, and are mentored in the art of ninja combat by the wise rat Splinter. When the evil Shredder attempts to take over the world, the turtles set out to stop him. Fun fact! Robin Williams, who was a big fan of the franchise, provided Judith Hoag with information regarding her character through his comic book collection; the two were co-starring in Cadillac Man when the Turtles film went into production.
A Goofy Movie (1995) When Max makes a preposterous promise to a girl he has a crush on, his chances to fulfilling it seem hopeless when he is dragged onto a cross-country trip with his embarrassing father, Goofy. Fun fact! Rob Paulsen (voice of P.J.) has admitted several times in interviews that he has never seen this film in its entirety.
The Witches (1990) A recently orphaned young boy is taken to a seaside hotel in England by his grandmother, who is recuperating after an illness.. Also staying at their hotel is a group of witches who have gathered to prepare a plot to rid England of all children. Fun fact! It took Anjelica Huston eight hours of make up time to transform into "The Grand High Witch".
Free Willy (1993) When a boy learns that a beloved killer whale is to be killed by the aquarium owners, the boy risks everything to free the whale. Not so fun fact! After the movie was released, it brought the living conditions of the star orca, Keiko (Willy) to the world's attention. His tank was too small, with too-warm chlorinated artificial salt water. Keiko suffered from a weakened immune system and a skin condition around his pectoral fins. There was a monumental effort to release Keiko. Unfortunately he did not fully adapt to the wild and died in 2003 shortly after release.
Flubber (1997) An eccentric inventor stumbles upon an innovative but highly unstable formula for mysterious rubber-like hybrid elastomer that can lift objects, bouncing and stretching as if it had a mind of its own. Of course, this unique but untamed creation catches the attention of a ruthless rival who is bent on getting his hands on the extraordinary substance. Fun fact! According to Wil Wheaton, in the scenes that he was in with Robin Williams, they would film a take the way it was supposed to be filmed. After that take, Williams would often want to improvise scenes differently than the script, just for fun. Those scenes were not added to the actual film, but there were enough scenes to make an entirely different movie.
Homeward Bound: The Incredible Journey (1993) A fun-loving American bulldog pup, a hilarious Himalayan cat, and a wise old golden retriever embark on a long trek through the rugged wilderness of the Sierra Nevada mountains in a quest to reach home and their beloved owners. Not so fun fact! This was one of Don Ameche's final movies before his death the year of the film's release. He voiced Shadow.
The Rescuers (1977) Two mice of the Rescue Aid Society search for a little girl kidnapped by unscrupulous treasure hunters. Fun fact! The film's popularity almost led to a spin-off TV series in 1989. When the animation department green-lit The Rescuers Down Under (1990), the spin-off was reworked into Chip 'n' Dale Rescue Rangers (1988), with Chip and Dale replacing Bernard and Miss Bianca.
Edward Scissorhands (1990) An artificial man, who was incompletely constructed and has scissors for hands, leads a solitary life. Then one day, a suburban lady meets him and introduces him to her world. Fun fact! The idea for the movie was inspired by a drawing Tim Burton had done when he was a teenager. He explains the character represented him as he was in his teenage years and how the film represents much of his time growing up. He likened growing up in Suburbia to a Frankenstein movie with the suburbanites acting as the angry villagers.
It Takes Two (1995) Alyssa and Amanda are two little girls who are identical, but complete strangers, that accidentally meet one day. Fun fact! The camp used in the film was Camp Mini-Yo-We located in Muskoka, Ontario, which is still an active summer camp today.
The Land Before Time (1988) An orphaned brontosaurus named Littlefoot sets off in search of the legendary Great Valley. A land of lush vegetation where the dinosaurs can thrive and live in peace. Along the way he meets four other young dinosaurs, each one a different species, and they encounter several obstacles and the evil predator Sharptooth as they learn to work together in order to survive. Fun fact! It was the highest grossing animated film of all time at he time of its release.
Home Alone (1990) An eight-year-old troublemaker must protect his house from a pair of burglars when he is accidentally left home alone by his family during Christmas vacation. Fun fact! Joe Pesci deliberately avoided Macaulay Culkin on-set, because he wanted Culkin to think he was mean.
Mars Attacks! (1996) It is a normal day for everyone, until President of the United States James Dale (Jack Nicholson) announces Martians have been spotted circling Earth. The Martians land and a meeting is arranged, but not everything goes to plan, and the Martians seem to have other plans for Earth. Are they just misunderstood beings or do they really want to destroy all of humanity? Fun fact! To make the Martian Girl costume as smooth as possible, it had no zipper or buttons. Lisa Marie had to be sewn into it every day.
Quest for Camelot (1998) During the times of King Arthur, the story of an adventurous brave girl, named Kayley, whose father, a Knight of the Round Table, is killed by Sir Ruber, a maniacal brute who steals Excalibur and ultimately threatens to seize King Arthur's Camelot. Kayley enlists the blind, reclusive knight-aspirant Garrett and a goofy two headed dragon to brave the Enchanted Forest and retrieve the magic sword. Fun fact! The original director of this movie, intended to make a darker movie, more faithful in tone to the original book. Following the phenomenal successes of the movies of the Disney Renaissance, Warner Brothers, hoped to replicate similar successes with their own animated movies. The original vision for this movie was rejected in favor of a more Disney animated musical.
The Nightmare Before Christmas (1993) Jack Skellington, the pumpkin king of Halloween Town, is bored with doing the same thing every year for Halloween. One day he stumbles into Christmas Town, and is so taken with the idea of Christmas that he tries to get the resident bats, ghouls, and goblins of Halloween Town to help him put on Christmas instead of Halloween. Fun fact! It took a group of around 100 people three years to complete this movie. For one second of film, up to 12 stop-motion moves had to be made.
Hook (1991) When Captain James Hook kidnaps his children, an adult Peter Pan must return to Neverland and reclaim his youthful spirit in order to challenge his old enemy. Fun fact! Dame Maggie Smith, being only 56 years old at the time of filming, was aged by make-up to play 92-year-old Granny Wendy. (I honestly wondered why Maggie Smith has been 'old' since I was born.)
Babe (1995) Babe, a pig raised by sheepdogs on a rural English farm, learns to herd sheep with a little help from Farmer Hoggett. Fun fact! Because baby pigs grow so fast, 48 pigs were used during filming for the role of Babe.
The Secret Garden (1993) A young, recently-orphaned girl is sent to England after living in India all of her life. Once there, she begins to explore her new, seemingly-isolated surroundings, and its secrets. Fun fact! The garden was built from scratch to avoid the logistical nightmare of crowd control that would have been required for a complicated summer and winter shoot in an existing garden.
The Princess Bride (1987) While home sick in bed, a young boy's grandfather reads him the story of a farmboy-turned-pirate who encounters numerous obstacles, enemies and allies in his quest to be reunited with his true love. Fun fact! According to author William Goldman, when he was first trying to get the movie made in the 1970s, a then-unknown Arnold Schwarzenegger wanted to play Fezzik, and he was strongly being considered because Goldman could never get his first choice, André René Roussimoff, to read for the role. By the time the movie was made about twelve years later, Schwarzenegger was such a big star they could not afford him. Andre was cast after all, and the two big men had gone on to become friends.
A Little Princess (1995) A young girl is relegated to servitude at a boarding school when her father goes missing and is presumed dead. Fun fact! Like the character she played, Liesel Matthews (Sara) came from a very wealthy background. After a family dispute and lawsuit in her early twenties, Liesel received about $500 million of her family's fortune. She subsequently became a philanthropist in, among other places, India (a country that her character Sara loved as well) and created several educational and development charities.
Mio in the Land of Faraway (1987) A lonely boy who is transferred from his dull life with his adoptive parents to the land where his real father is the King. In that country, he sets out on a quest, together with his new friend, to destroy the evil Knight Kato. Fun fact! The Chernobyl power plant disaster occurred when this movie was being shot. The crew evacuated the area completely, but came back to shoot the next month. They had numerous Geiger counters set throughout the shoot to monitor radiation levels.
Beetlejuice (1988) The spirits of a deceased couple are harassed by an unbearable family that has moved into their home, and hire a malicious spirit to drive them out. Fun fact! According to Michael Keaton, the Beetlejuice character was described to him by director Tim Burton as "having lived in every time period but no time period." Keaton used this as the jumping-off point to create the character with such features as a shock hairdo, mold make-up, and large teeth. Keaton ad libbed 90% of his lines.
Pee-wee's Big Adventure (1985) When eccentric man-child Pee-wee Herman gets his beloved bike stolen in broad daylight, he sets out across the U.S. on the adventure of his life. Fun fact! During the entire time Large Marge tells her story, she never blinks.
Gremlins (1984) A young man inadvertently breaks three important rules concerning his new pet and unleashes a horde of malevolently mischievous monsters on a small town. Fun fact! Zach Galligan recounted all of the Gremlins were animatronics, each costing near $40,000. When everyone left the lot for the day, security would have everyone open the trunks of their cars to make sure they weren't stolen.
The Great Mouse Detective (1986) Basil, the rodent Sherlock Holmes, investigates the kidnapping of a toy maker and uncovers its link to his archenemy, Professor Ratigan. Fun fact! During the recording of Vincent Price's lines, animators sketched his exaggerated Shakespearean gestures and worked them into the animated poses for Ratigan.
Doggo tax! ♥ This is by no means a complete list, but just something that includes my personal most remembered tapes. Hope you enjoyed, now grow old and die with me. ^_^
item.dc:creator: Xandelle

item.title: If we are going to argue all day on the internets, let’s get pedantic!
item.dc:creator: memepawn

item.title: To The Scottish Gal On Tinder, Thank You!
item.description: She had a 12 hour layover in my city, took a risk on Tinder, and met up with a gorilla. She was gorgeous with curly red hair and was lots of fun! Amy, I know we'll never see each other again and you'll probably never see this, but that was a hell of a night and morning! Thank you! And to the Universe, Thanks I needed the self esteem boost.
item.dc:creator: Beattywill80

item.title: 'Murica. Nah, just sleep in the street nigga. and be quiet about it.
item.description: Hating 'murica is so easy
